content:
# Colossians Chapter 3 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/COL_003_page_001.jpg, COL_003_page_002.jpg

1. If therefore you were raised with the Christos, seek the things above, where the Christos is, sitting at [the] right [hand] of God.

2. Set your mind on the things above, not on the things upon the earth.

3. For you died, and your life has been hidden with the Christos in God.

4. When the Christos, your life, is revealed, then you also will be revealed with him in glory.

5. Put to death therefore the members which [are] upon the earth: sexual immorality, impurity, passion, evil desire, and the greediness, which is idolatry;

6. because of which the wrath of God comes upon the sons of disobedience;

7. in which you also once walked, when you were living in these things.

8. But now you also, put off all [these]: anger, wrath, malice, slander, obscene language out of your mouth.

9. Do not lie to one another, having put off the old man with his practices,

10. and having put on the new [man], the [one] being renewed unto knowledge according to [the] image of the [one] having created him,

11. where there is no Greek and Jew, circumcision and uncircumcision, barbarian, Scythian, slave, free, but Christos [is] all and in all.

12. Put on therefore, as chosen [ones] of God, holy and beloved, bowels of compassion, kindness, humility, gentleness, patience,

13. bearing with one another and forgiving one another, if anyone has a complaint against anyone; just as also the Lord forgave you, so also you.

14. And above all these things [put on] love, which is [the] bond of the completeness.

15. And let the peace of the Christos rule in your hearts, unto which also you were called in one body; and become thankful.

16. Let the word of the Christos dwell in you richly, in all wisdom teaching and admonishing one another, in psalms, hymns, spiritual songs, in grace singing in your hearts to God.

17. And everything, whatever you do in word or in deed, [do] all in [the] name of [the] Lord Yeshua, giving thanks to God [the] Father through him.

18. Wives, submit to the husbands, as is fitting in [the] Lord.

19. Husbands, love the wives and do not be bitter against them.

20. Children, obey the parents in all things, for this is well-pleasing in [the] Lord.

21. Fathers, do not provoke your children, that they may not be discouraged.

22. Slaves, obey in all things the lords according to [the] flesh, not with eye-service as people-pleasers, but in singleness of heart, fearing the Lord.

23. Whatever you do, work from [the] soul, as to the Lord and not to men,

24. knowing that from [the] Lord you will receive the reward of the inheritance; you serve the Lord Christos.

25. For the [one] doing wrong will receive back the wrong he did, and there is no partiality.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.3 — "your life has been hidden":** kekryptai — perfect passive: "has been hidden and remains hidden." The believer's true life is concealed with Christ in God — a present hiddenness awaiting future revelation (v.4).

2. **v.11 — The pairs dissolved:** Paul lists five pairs/categories that are abolished in Christ: Greek/Jew, circumcision/uncircumcision, barbarian, Scythian, slave/free. The Scythians were considered the most barbarous — their inclusion emphasizes the total dissolution of ethnic hierarchy.

3. **v.14 — "the bond of the completeness":** syndesmos tēs teleiotētos — love is the binding force that completes the other virtues. syndesmos = "bond/ligament" — the same word used for the body's ligaments in 2:19.

4. **v.16 — "the word of the Christos":** ho logos tou Christou — unique phrase (elsewhere Paul says "word of God" or "word of the Lord"). Here the word that Christ spoke, or the message about Christ, is to dwell richly in the community.

— EMET, AI Translator
